name: Smartphone
description: A modern phone’s materials loop — mining through e-waste — and where reuse/refurbish beats shredding.

stages.details: Copper, gold, lithium, cobalt, rare earths and silica are extracted worldwide. Ore processing and refining are energy- and water-intensive, often with toxic tailings.
Semiconductor fabs, display plants, and battery factories feed contract assembly. Design for thinness and sealed batteries makes later repair harder.

stages.summary: Global logistics to retail and users
stages.details: Air and sea freight move devices to carriers and stores. Frequent model launches drive return flows of barely-used stock.
Phones in drawers or informal dumps leak metals and plastics. Formal take-back is incomplete; informal recycling can expose workers to toxins.
Trade-in, certified refurbished, battery/screen repair, and software support keep devices useful. Modular design and spare parts amplify this loop.
Specialized recyclers shred and separate fractions; smelting recovers precious metals. Plastics and mixed fractions are harder; battery chemistries need dedicated lines.
